steps.title: Identify the physiological test for the critic's claim
steps.content: The critic argues the lullaby decreased heart rate by capturing attention, not relaxing the infants. The passage establishes that pupils enlarge when a stimulus captures attention. So pupil size becomes the direct measure of whether the critic's mechanism actually engaged.

steps.title: Apply the attention test to the choices
steps.content: Smaller pupils during the lullaby indicate the lullaby captured less attention than the non-lullaby—directly refuting the critic's explanation. The other choices either reference signals the passage never identifies (choice A), or provide background facts unrelated to attention response (choices B and D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
